channel.title: Rountree Leitman Klein & Geer, LLC Blog
channel.description: Expert legal insights on bankruptcy, litigation, real estate law, and creditor rights from Atlanta's trusted attorneys.

image.title: Rountree Leitman Klein & Geer, LLC Blog

item.title: All Five RLKG Partners Named 2026 Georgia Super Lawyers
item.description: All five partners at Atlanta's Rountree Leitman Klein & Geer have been named 2026 Georgia Super Lawyers — a designation earned by the top 5% of attorneys statewide.

item.title: Planning Spring Break? Don't Charge Your Vacation Before Filing Bankruptcy
item.description: With spring break approaching, learn why charging a vacation on your credit card within 90 days of filing bankruptcy could make that debt non-dischargeable under Section 523 of the Bankruptcy Code.

item.title: RLKG Law's Winter 2026 Charitable Contributions
item.description: RLKG Law is proud to support seven outstanding organizations making a difference in our community this winter, including NAMI Georgia, Camp Kudzu, and Open Hand Atlanta.

item.title: Are Debts Discharged in Bankruptcy Considered Taxable Income?
item.description: Learn whether debts discharged in bankruptcy are considered taxable income. Understand 1099-C forms and IRS Form 982 to protect yourself from improper tax assessments.

item.title: What Does It Mean To Have "Equity"?
item.description: Understanding equity is crucial in bankruptcy. Learn how home equity affects your Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 options.

item.title: What If My Income Goes Down During My Chapter 13 Case?
item.description: Learn what happens if your income decreases during Chapter 13 bankruptcy. You may be able to modify your plan payments.

item.title: How Much Does Chapter 13 Bankruptcy Cost?
item.description: Understand the costs of Chapter 13 bankruptcy in Georgia. Learn about attorney fees, filing fees, and how most fees are paid through your repayment plan.

item.title: Is There a Good Time to File for Bankruptcy?
item.description: Timing matters when filing bankruptcy. Learn the best time to file and how strategic timing can maximize your benefits.

item.title: How to Use Bankruptcy to Lower Your Car Payment
item.description: Learn how Chapter 13 bankruptcy cramdown provisions can help reduce your car payment in Georgia.

item.title: What Bills You Should Still Pay After Filing Bankruptcy
item.description: Not all debts stop after filing bankruptcy. Learn which bills you should continue paying.

item.title: The Foreclosure Process in Georgia
item.description: Understand the foreclosure process in Georgia and how bankruptcy can help stop or delay foreclosure.

item.title: How Bankruptcy Can Improve Your Credit Score
item.description: Contrary to popular belief, filing bankruptcy can actually help improve your credit score over time.

item.title: What Every Parent Should Know About Bankruptcy
item.description: A comprehensive guide for parents considering bankruptcy in Georgia.

item.title: 3 Things to Remember About Credit Card Use Before Filing Bankruptcy
item.description: Important guidelines on credit card use before filing bankruptcy in Georgia.

item.title: The 3 Most Common Reasons Why People File Bankruptcy
item.description: Discover the three most common reasons people file bankruptcy: divorce, job loss, and medical bills.

item.title: Bankruptcy and Divorce: A Guide for Family Lawyers and Consumers
item.description: Bankruptcy can have a profound impact on family law proceedings. Learn what bankruptcy can and cannot do for family lawyers and consumers facing divorce.

item.title: RLKG Recognized for Back-to-School Contribution
item.description: Rountree Leitman Klein & Geer was honored to participate in this year's back-to-school drive, supporting local students and families.

item.title: Buying a Home After Bankruptcy in Georgia
item.description: Many worry that bankruptcy will permanently prevent homeownership. Learn how to rebuild credit and qualify for a mortgage after bankruptcy.

item.title: Georgia Enacts Sweeping Tort Reform in 2025
item.description: Governor Brian Kemp signed landmark tort reform legislation marking the most significant changes to Georgia's civil justice system in decades.

item.title: David S. Klein Speaks on Forms of Title Litigation
item.description: Partner David S. Klein delivered a presentation at the State Bar of Georgia Real Property Law Section's Title Standards Seminar.

item.title: RLKG Attorneys Receive Super Lawyers Honors for 2025
item.description: Partners William A. Rountree, Hal J. Leitman, Will B. Geer, and Michael J. Bargar have been recognized as 2025 Georgia Super Lawyers.

item.title: How Can I Get Rid of a FIFA (Writ of Fieri Facias) in Bankruptcy?
item.description: Learn how bankruptcy can help you eliminate a Writ of Fieri Facias (FIFA) lien on your property in Georgia.

item.title: Individuals Can File Chapter 11 to Protect Assets and Reorganize Finances
item.description: Chapter 11 is not just for businesses. Learn how individuals can use Chapter 11 bankruptcy to protect assets and reorganize their finances.

item.title: Partner David S. Klein Shares Insights on Title Litigation at State Bar Seminar
item.description: David S. Klein spoke at the State Bar of Georgia Real Property Law Section's Title Standards CLE Seminar on February 27, 2024.

item.title: Five Attorneys Selected as 2024 Georgia Super Lawyers
item.description: Rountree Leitman Klein & Geer proudly announces that five partners have been honored as 2024 Georgia Super Lawyers.

item.title: What Every Parent Should Know About Bankruptcy
item.description: Filing bankruptcy has significant ramifications on 529 college savings plans. Learn what every parent should know about protecting their children's education funds.

item.title: Rountree Leitman Klein & Geer Hosts Charity Event for The Sandwich Project
item.description: RLKG hosted a charitable event at its office on December 10, 2023, to benefit The Sandwich Project, helping to alleviate hunger in our community.

item.title: Are My Wages Being Garnished?
item.description: Having your wages garnished is one of the most common reasons people seek bankruptcy. Learn how bankruptcy can stop wage garnishment.

item.title: Filing a Proof of Claim in a Bankruptcy Case
item.description: Filing a proof of claim is an important step for creditors in a bankruptcy case. Learn about the requirements and deadlines for filing.

item.title: Partners Speak at Annual Residential Real Estate Seminar
item.description: Partners David S. Klein and Michael J. Bargar spoke at the State Bar of Georgia's Annual Residential Real Estate Seminar on February 9, 2023.

item.title: Four Rountree Leitman Klein & Geer Attorneys Selected to Georgia Super Lawyers List
item.description: Four partners selected to the Georgia Super Lawyers list for 2023, with David Klein selected as a Rising Star.

item.title: Understanding Quiet Title Actions in Real Estate Law
item.description: A quiet title action is a legal procedure used to resolve disputes over property ownership. Learn when and how quiet title actions are used.

item.title: Should My Business File Chapter 11? Ask Yourself These Five Questions
item.description: Will Geer discusses the five questions a business should ask when determining whether to file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.

item.title: How to Qualify for a Mortgage After Bankruptcy
item.description: Contrary to popular belief, filing bankruptcy is not going to destroy your credit for life. Learn how to qualify for a mortgage after bankruptcy.

item.title: Transfer Title Before Filing Bankruptcy? Get Ready for a Rough Ride
item.description: Learn why transferring property before filing bankruptcy can lead to serious legal consequences.

item.title: How Long Will My Chapter 7 Case Last?
item.description: Learn how long a typical Chapter 7 bankruptcy case takes from filing to discharge.

item.title: Will Geer Discusses Finances and Bankruptcy
item.description: In this informational video, Will Geer discusses finances and bankruptcy including the right time to file and the benefits of filing.

item.title: Easements, Covenants, Leases, and Licenses: Top Misconceptions Clarified
item.description: Clarifying common misconceptions about easements, covenants, leases, and licenses in Georgia real estate law.

item.title: Can You Keep Your Property in Bankruptcy?
item.description: There is a common misconception that bankruptcy requires debtors to sell off most of their assets. Learn about Georgia bankruptcy exemptions.

item.title: Factors That Could Deny Your Discharge
item.description: For many debtors, a successful bankruptcy filing results in the removal of crushing debt. However, there are several reasons courts may deny your discharge.

item.title: The Automatic Stay and Relief from Creditors
item.description: Learn about the automatic stay in bankruptcy and how it provides crucial protection from creditors taking action against you.

item.title: CARES II Permits PPP Loans and Other Relief
item.description: Learn how the CARES II Act allows Subchapter V debtors to apply for PPP loans and provides other relief for small businesses in bankruptcy.
